questions.question: According to the dual process model of coping with bereavement, healthy grieving involves:

options.text: Resolving all grief within the first year after a loss
Oscillating between confronting emotional pain and attending to practical life changes
Moving through the five stages of grief in a fixed sequence
Suppressing grief and focusing exclusively on practical tasks

questions.explanation: The dual process model, proposed by Stroebe and Schut, describes healthy grieving as oscillating between loss-oriented coping (processing emotional pain) and restoration-oriented coping (managing practical changes). This model recognizes that bereaved people need both mourning and rebuilding.

questions.question: Which criticism of the Kubler-Ross model of dying is most supported by current research?

options.text: The five stages are experienced in a fixed, linear order by all dying individuals
The model ignores the existence of denial as a coping mechanism
The model only applies to people with terminal cancer
The grief process is more variable and individual than the stage model suggests

questions.explanation: Research has shown that the grief process is highly individual and does not follow a fixed sequence of stages. Not everyone experiences all five stages, and people may move between stages or experience several simultaneously. Kubler-Ross herself acknowledged this variability.
